title: Pasania sootepensis
taxonomy: Pasania sootepensis belongs to the family Lauraceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the laurel family. This family consists of about 2000 species, with many being native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. The genus Pasania is a part of the subfamily Lauroideae and is characterized by its distinctive leaf morphology. Pasania sootepensis is a species of flowering plant endemic to Thailand, and its classification also includes its botanical name. The exact classification of Pasania sootepensis can be further broken down into its kingdom (Plantae), phylum (Angiospermophyta), and other taxonomic ranks.
uses: Pasania sootepensis has been reported to have various medicinal properties. It is traditionally used in Southeast Asia to treat fever and other health issues. intro: Pasania sootepensis, also known as the Thai teak, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lauraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Thai folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pasania sootepensis include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as terpenoids and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pasania sootepensis is often prepared as a decoction or infused in hot water, and is used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
